labs.name: anticardiolipin antibody IgG
labs.note: positive; increased from 48 GPL on initial testing

labs.name: lupus anticoagulant by dilute Russell viper venom time (dRVVT)
labs.note: more clearly positive than initial testing

labs.name: anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I antibody IgG
labs.note: positive; increased from 14 units, now above positivity threshold

conditions.name: antiphospholipid syndrome, obstetric subtype
conditions.note: Meets revised Sapporo (Sydney) classification criteria based on three consecutive unexplained early pregnancy losses before 10 weeks gestation combined with persistently positive anticardiolipin IgG at moderate titer, persistently positive lupus anticoagulant, and now-positive anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I IgG confirmed at greater than 12 weeks from initial testing. Risk stratification: medium risk (not triple-positive at high titer).

conditions.name: recurrent pregnancy loss
conditions.note: Known condition from prior encounters. Now attributed to antiphospholipid syndrome.

medications.name: aspirin 81 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 81 mg by mouth once daily, to begin preconception when patient is ready to attempt conception
medications.reason: antiphospholipid syndrome, obstetric subtype — preconception thromboprophylaxis

medications.name: enoxaparin 40 mg subcutaneous injection
medications.sig: Inject 40 mg subcutaneously once daily, to initiate upon positive pregnancy test (prophylactic dose)
medications.reason: antiphospholipid syndrome, obstetric subtype — pregnancy anticoagulation prophylaxis

document_references.description: Comprehensive hematology follow-up note documenting formal confirmation of antiphospholipid syndrome (obstetric subtype) based on confirmatory 12-week repeat antibody testing meeting revised Sapporo/Sydney criteria. Includes detailed risk stratification (medium risk, not triple-positive, thrombophilia screening negative), complete anticoagulation protocol for future pregnancy (preconception aspirin, enoxaparin upon pregnancy, peripartum management, postpartum anticoagulation), long-term APS management recommendations, patient counseling on prognosis, and coordination plan with Dr. Whitfield (MFM) and Dr. Forsythe (OB/GYN).

observation_names: lupus anticoagulant by dilute Russell viper venom time (dRVVT)
